entries.content: I'll start by reading the spec and investigating the codebase thoroughly before creating the plan.

entries.detail:
1→# AC implementation plan progress in Quick Look + CLI
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→Auto-Claude's `implementation_plan.json` has per-phase subtask state
     6→(pending / in_progress / completed) that gives a clear picture of how far
     7→along a job is. Neither the Quick Look card preview nor `bop list` surface
     8→this — only raw `progress: Int` from `meta.json` is shown. The goal is to
     9→pipe AC's phase/subtask tree into the card's visual layer with zero extra
    10→tooling: block progress bars in the CLI, and a dedicated "Plan" tab in Quick
    11→Look alongside the existing Subtasks tab.
    12→
    13→## Data model
    14→
    15→### 1. `ac_spec_id` field in `Meta`
    16→
    17→Add to `crates/bop-core/src/lib.rs`:
    18→
    19→```rust
    20→pub ac_spec_id: Option<String>,   // e.g. "022"
    21→```
    22→
    23→This is the only link needed. Quick Look and CLI resolve the full path:
    24→
    25→```
    26→<git_root>/.auto-claude/specs/<ac_spec_id>-*/implementation_plan.json
    27→```
    28→
    29→Use a glob search (`read_dir` + prefix match on `<ac_spec_id>-`) to find the
    30→spec dir — do not hardcode the slug.
    31→
    32→### 2. How `ac_spec_id` gets written
    33→
    34→Two entry points:
    35→
    36→**dispatch.nu** — when `write_approval` fires before spawning a pane, also
    37→search `.cards/` (all state dirs) for a card whose `meta.json` `id` matches
    38→the spec slug or whose `title` contains the spec ID. If found, write
    39→`ac_spec_id` into that card's meta. This is the dogfood path where bop uses
    40→itself to track its own development.
    41→
    42→**Adapter convention** — if an adapter runs AC internally, it can write
    43→`ac_spec_id` to `meta.json` via `write_meta` before exiting.
    44→
    45→For cards with no AC spec, `ac_spec_id` is absent — no change to rendering.
    46→
    47→## CLI changes (`crates/bop-cli/src/main.rs` or status command)
    48→
    49→### `bop list` / `bop status` inline progress
    50→
    51→When a card has `ac_spec_id` set, load `implementation_plan.json` and render
    52→a compact one-liner after the card row:
    53→
    54→```
    55→⚙ 022-atomic-write-meta   running   claude   6m 14s
    56→  ████████████░░░░  6/7   Phase 2: CLI + Docs  ◑
    57→```
    58→
    59→Format:
    60→- Block bar: 16 chars, `█` per completed subtask fraction, `░` remainder
    61→- `N/T` count: completed subtasks / total subtasks across all phases
    62→- Current phase name (truncated to 24 chars)
    63→- Phase completion half-circle: `○◔◑◕●` (0% 25% 50% 75% 100%)
    64→  - U+25CB, U+25D4, U+25D1, U+25D5, U+25CF — all BMP-safe
    65→
    66→Half-circle thresholds (fraction of subtasks in current phase done):
    67→- 0 % → `○`
    68→- 1–33 % → `◔`
    69→- 34–66 % → `◑`
    70→- 67–99 % → `◕`
    71→- 100 % → `●`
    72→
    73→Colors: completed subtasks = green, in_progress = amber, pending = dim.
    74→Only shown for cards where `ac_spec_id` resolves to a readable file.
    75→
    76→### `bop status --watch` (spec 025)
    77→
    78→When the plan view is live, also reload `implementation_plan.json` on each
    79→notify event — it changes as the AC agent commits subtasks.
    80→
    81→## Quick Look changes (`macos/bop/PreviewViewController.swift`)
    82→
    83→### New tab: "Plan"
    84→
    85→Add `plan = "Plan"` to the `CardSection` enum (alongside `subtasks`).
    86→Show the tab only if `ac_spec_id` is set and `implementation_plan.json` loads.
    87→
    88→The Plan tab reads `implementation_plan.json` from the git root (walk up from
    89→the card dir via `../` until `.git` or `.auto-claude` is found).
    90→
    91→### New Codable structs
    92→
    93→```swift
    94→private struct AcPlan: Codable {
    95→    let phases: [AcPhase]
    96→}
    97→
    98→private struct AcPhase: Codable {
    99→    let id: String
   100→    let name: String
   101→    let subtasks: [AcSubtask]
   102→}
   103→
   104→private struct AcSubtask: Codable {
   105→    let id: String
   106→    let description: String
   107→    let status: String   // "pending" | "in_progress" | "completed"
   108→}
   109→```
   110→
   111→### Plan tab rendering
   112→
   113→Overall progress bar at top (same style as existing `subtasksTab` header):
   114→
   115→```
   116→8 of 12 subtasks complete          67%
   117→████████████░░░░░░░░
   118→```
   119→
   120→Then per-phase collapsible rows:
   121→
   122→```
   123→◉  Phase 1 — Crash Safety          ●●●●●  5/5
   124→   ✓ Make write_meta atomic
   125→   ✓ Make write_providers atomic
   126→   ✓ Add recover_orphans function
   127→   ✓ Call recover_orphans on startup
   128→   ✓ Add bop recover CLI command
   129→
   130→◔  Phase 2 — CLI + Docs            ●○○    1/3
   131→   ✓ Write result.md (in_progress)
   132→   ○ Run make check
   133→   ○ Final review
   134→```
   135→
   136→Glyph legend (BMP-safe, existing SF Symbols for Quick Look):
   137→- Phase status icon: `circle.fill` (done), `circle.lefthalf.filled` (partial),
   138→  `circle` (not started) — SF Symbols, no font dep
   139→- Subtask row: `checkmark.circle.fill` (completed, green),
   140→  `arrow.triangle.2.circlepath` (in_progress, amber), `circle` (pending, dim)
   141→  — matches the existing `subtasksTab` icon style
   142→
   143→Phase name row background: match existing card row style
   144→(`Color.black.opacity(0.15)` + `RoundedRectangle` border).
   145→
   146→### `BopCardMeta` addition
   147→
   148→```swift
   149→let acSpecId: String?
   150→
   151→enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
   152→    // ... existing keys ...
   153→    case acSpecId = "ac_spec_id"
   154→}
   155→```
   156→
   157→Load `AcPlan` lazily when the Plan tab is first shown — do not block the
   158→initial card render. Use a `@State var acPlan: AcPlan? = nil` loaded in
   159→`.task { }` on the tab view.
   160→
   161→Git root discovery: walk parent dirs from `cardURL.deletingLastPathComponent()`
   162→until a dir containing `.auto-claude` is found, max 6 levels. Cache the result.
   163→
   164→## Acceptance
   165→
   166→- `Meta` has `ac_spec_id: Option<String>` field (serde rename `ac_spec_id`)
   167→- `bop list` renders block bar + phase half-circle for cards with `ac_spec_id`
   168→- `bop status --watch` reloads plan on file changes
   169→- Quick Look shows "Plan" tab when `ac_spec_id` resolves to a readable plan
   170→- Plan tab shows overall block bar + per-phase subtask list with SF Symbol icons
   171→- Git root discovery walks up from card dir (max 6 levels)
   172→- `make check` passes
   173→- `output/result.md` with ASCII screenshot of CLI output and description of QL tab
   174→

1→[workspace]
     2→resolver = "2"
     3→members = [
     4→  "crates/bop-core",
     5→  "crates/bop-cli",
     6→]
     7→# bop-zellij-plugin is a WASM-only cdylib and must be built separately:
     8→#   cargo build -p bop-zellij-plugin --target wasm32-wasip1 --release
     9→# It cannot be compiled for the host target (cdylib + WASM-only deps).
    10→
    11→[workspace.package]
    12→edition = "2021"
    13→license = "MIT"
    14→
    15→[workspace.dependencies]
    16→anyhow = "1"
    17→chrono = { version = "0.4", features = ["serde"] }
    18→clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
    19→clap_complete = "4"
    20→serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
    21→serde_json = "1"
    22→thiserror = "2"
    23→walkdir = "2"
    24→url = "2"
    25→
    26→dirs = "5.0"
    27→
    28→[profile.release]
    29→opt-level = 3
    30→lto = "thin"
    31→

1→.PHONY: test build clean lint
     2→
     3→# Build the project
     4→build:
     5→	cargo build
     6→
     7→# Run tests
     8→test:
     9→	cargo test
    10→
    11→# Run clippy for linting
    12→lint:
    13→	cargo clippy -- -D warnings
    14→
    15→# Clean build artifacts
    16→clean:
    17→	cargo clean
    18→
    19→# Format code
    20→fmt:
    21→	cargo fmt
    22→
    23→# Check formatting
    24→check-fmt:
    25→	cargo fmt --check
    26→
    27→# Run all checks
    28→check: test lint check-fmt
    29→
    30→# Install to system
    31→install: build
    32→	sudo cp target/debug/bop /usr/local/bin/bop
    33→
    34→# Development setup
    35→dev:
    36→	cargo watch -x 'run -- bop dispatcher --once'
    37→

150→        id: String,
   151→    },
   152→    /// List cards with glyphs, stages, and progress.
   153→    List {
   154→        /// Filter: pending, running, done, failed, merged, active (default), all.
   155→        #[arg(long, default_value = "active")]
   156→        state: String,
   157→        /// Emit newline-delimited JSON (ndjson) instead of ANSI table.
   158→        /// One JSON object per line: Meta fields + "state" + "team".
   159→        /// Pipe to: jq, nu, fzf, rg for filtering and selection.
   160→        #[arg(long)]
   161→        json: bool,
   162→    },
   163→    /// Safely mutate selected meta fields with schema validation.
   164→    Meta {
   165→        #[command(subcommand)]
   166→        action: MetaAction,
   167→    },
   168→    /// Run policy gates.
   169→    Policy {
   170→        #[command(subcommand)]
   171→        action: PolicyAction,
   172→    },
   173→    /// Check local toolchain/environment prerequisites.
   174→    Doctor {
   175→        /// Skip time-consuming checks (e.g. make check).
   176→        #[arg(long)]
   177→        fast: bool,
   178→    },
   179→    /// Generate shell completion script.
   180→    Completions {
   181→        #[arg(value_enum)]
   182→        shell: clap_complete::Shell,
   183→    },
   184→    /// Async planning-poker estimation using playing-card glyphs.
   185→    Poker {
   186→        #[command(subcommand)]
   187→        action: PokerAction,
   188→    },
   189→    /// Manage launchd services for dispatcher and merge-gate.
   190→    Factory {
   191→        #[command(subcommand)]
   192→        action: FactoryAction,
   193→    },
   194→    /// Keep Finder folder icons in sync with card state.
   195→    Icons {
   196→        #[command(subcommand)]
   197→        action: IconsAction,
   198→    },
   199→    /// Promote cards from drafts/ to pending/, making them eligible for dispatch.
   200→    Promote {
   201→        /// Card ID, or "all" to promote every draft.
   202→        id: String,
   203→    },
   204→    /// Import cards from a JSON file into drafts/ (or pending/ with --immediate).
   205→    Import {
   206→        /// Path to JSON file with card definitions (a JSON array of card objects).
   207→        source: String,
   208→        /// Import directly to pending/ instead of drafts/.
   209→        #[arg(long)]
   210→        immediate: bool,
   211→    },
   212→    /// Generate a concise codebase map at .cards/CODEBASE.md for agent orientation.
   213→    Index {
   214→        /// Print to stdout instead of writing the file.
   215→        #[arg(long)]
   216→        print: bool,
   217→    },
   218→    /// Quick-create an ideation card from a topic string.
   219→    #[command(alias = "brainstorm", alias = "ideation")]
   220→    Bstorm {
   221→        /// Topic words (joined into the spec & slugified into the card ID).
   222→        topic: Vec<String>,
   223→        /// Team for glyph suit assignment (cli, arch, quality, platform).
   224→        #[arg(long)]
   225→        team: Option<String>,
   226→    },
   227→    /// Show a Gantt timeline of card runs (ANSI by default, --html for browser).
   228→    Gantt {
   229→        /// Output HTML file instead of ANSI terminal chart.
   230→        #[arg(long)]
   231→        html: bool,
   232→        /// Open the HTML file in the default browser (implies --html).
   233→        #[arg(long, short = 'o')]
   234→        open: bool,

1→#!/usr/bin/env nu
     2→# dispatch.nu — bop spec dispatcher
     3→#
     4→# Runs Auto-Claude specs in dependency-ordered waves with rate-limit-aware
     5→# pacing. Spawns Zellij panes in the current session.
     6→#
     7→# Usage:
     8→#   nu dispatch.nu plan              # Show full wave plan, no execution
     9→#   nu dispatch.nu run --wave 0      # Execute only Wave 0
    10→#   nu dispatch.nu run               # Execute all pending waves
    11→#   nu dispatch.nu run --yes         # Skip confirmation prompts
    12→#   nu dispatch.nu status            # Show what's done / pending / failed
    13→#   nu dispatch.nu reset --spec 001  # Mark a spec as pending again
    14→#   nu dispatch.nu mark-done 001     # Manually mark complete
    15→#   nu dispatch.nu mark-failed 001   # Manually mark failed
    16→#   nu dispatch.nu roadmap           # Spawn roadmap agent in Zellij pane
    17→#   nu dispatch.nu ideate            # Spawn ideation agent in Zellij pane
    18→
    19→const PYTHON      = "/Applications/Auto-Claude.app/Contents/Resources/python/bin/python3"
    20→const SITE_PKGS   = "/Applications/Auto-Claude.app/Contents/Resources/python-site-packages"
    21→const BACKEND     = "/Applications/Auto-Claude.app/Contents/Resources/backend"
    22→const PROJECT_DIR = "/Users/studio/bop"
    23→const STATE_FILE  = "/Users/studio/bop/.auto-claude/dispatch-state.json"
    24→const LOCK_FILE   = "/Users/studio/bop/.auto-claude/dispatch-lock.json"
    25→
    26→def zellij_session [] {
    27→  if "ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME" in $env {
    28→    $env.ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME
    29→  } else {
    30→    # Not inside Zellij — find the single active session, or error
    31→    let sessions = (^zellij list-sessions --no-formatting 2>/dev/null | lines | where { |l| ($l | str trim) != "" })
    32→    if ($sessions | length) == 1 {
    33→      $sessions | first | str trim
    34→    } else if ($sessions | length) > 1 {
    35→      error make { msg: $"Multiple Zellij sessions active: ($sessions | str join ', '). Set ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME or run from inside a session." }
    36→    } else {
    37→      error make { msg: "No Zellij session found. Start one with: zellij --session bop" }
    38→    }
    39→  }
    40→}
    41→
    42→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    43→# Spec table
    44→# wave: 0=no-deps direct, 1=sequential, 2=parallel
    45→# cost: 1=trivial 2=small 3=medium 4=complex
    46→# mode: direct=writes to repo (no merge needed), isolated=worktree
    47→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    48→def specs [] {
    49→  [
    50→    # ── Wave 0: create test script, no deps ─────────────────────────────────
    51→    [id,    name,                                    wave, cost, mode    ];
    52→    ["001", "create scripts/test-real-adapters.nu",  0,    3,    "direct"],
    53→
    54→    # ── Wave 1: run each adapter e2e — sequential so fixes carry forward ────
    55→    ["002", "ollama end-to-end adapter test",         1,    3,    "direct"],
    56→    ["003", "claude end-to-end adapter test",         1,    4,    "direct"],
    57→    ["004", "codex end-to-end adapter test",          1,    3,    "direct"],
    58→
    59→    # ── Wave 2: team-cli P1 features (sequential — each builds on prev) ───────
    60→    ["006", "job-control: retry/kill/logs commands",  2,    4,    "direct"],
    61→    ["007", "bop clean command",                      2,    2,    "direct"],
    62→    ["008", "shell completions (bash/zsh/fish)",      2,    2,    "direct"],
    63→
    64→    # ── Wave 3: event-driven merge-gate (launchd/systemd.path, no daemon) ───
    65→    # TRIZ #13 Inversion: OS calls bop on done/ change; bop exits after --once
    66→    ["009", "event-driven merge-gate (launchd/systemd.path)", 3, 3, "direct"],
    67→
    68→    # ── Wave 4: foundation hardening — fix before adding more features ───────
    69→    ["010", "fix factory: KeepAlive → WatchPaths",     4, 2, "direct"],
    70→    ["011", "fix CI: replace self-hosted with ubuntu",  4, 2, "direct"],
    71→    ["012", "dogfood: seed bop's own backlog as cards", 4, 2, "direct"],
    72→
    73→    # ── Wave 5: VM-first execution — QEMU adapter (dub + zam) ────────────────
    74→    # Target: every card runs in an isolated VM, not on the host
    75→    # dub: /Users/studio/efi — UEFI bootloader + TPM attestation
    76→    # zam: /Users/studio/zam — <5MB unikernel, 9P card reader, UART output
    77→    ["013", "QEMU adapter: VM-per-card execution",     5, 4, "direct"],
    78→
    79→    # ── Wave 6: finish loose ends + bop serve smoke test ────────────────────
    80→    # Previously run via codex exec; now via run.py (--direct) so Auto-Claude
    81→    # subtask state is recorded. AC sees the already-committed work and closes out.
    82→    ["014", "make check clean: fix clippy + finish install-hooks Linux",  6, 2, "direct"],
    83→    ["015", "bop serve smoke test: curl POST creates card in pending/",   6, 3, "direct"],
    84→    ["016", "resume spec-003 last subtask: claude e2e cleanup",           6, 2, "direct"],
    85→
    86→    # ── Wave 8: laptop resilience — ACID safety + battery/wifi/cell ─────────
    87→    # Fixes: atomic write_meta, crash recovery, pause/resume, sleep/wake,
    88→    # network resilience, transient retry, live status watch.
    89→    # Sequential: 022 (atomics) must land before 023 (pause uses write_meta),
    90→    # 023 before 024 (sleep handler calls pause logic).
    91→    ["022", "atomic write_meta + bop recover",                            8, 3, "direct"],
    92→    ["023", "bop pause / resume / retry-transient",                       8, 3, "direct"],
    93→    ["024", "sleep/wake awareness + network resilience",                  8, 4, "direct"],
    94→    ["025", "bop status --watch (live in-place terminal view)",           8, 3, "direct"],
    95→    ["026", "storage resilience: JSONL WAL + checksum + file perms",     8, 3, "direct"],
    96→
    97→    # ── Wave 9: terminal card renderer + AC plan progress ────────────────────
    98→    # 028 first: renderer trait + TermCaps are the surface 027 renders into
    99→    ["028", "terminal card renderer: progressive degradation + demoscene",  9, 4, "direct"],
   100→
   101→    ["029", "bop ui: full TUI (ratatui, 3-pane, fuzzy, log tail)",        9, 4, "direct"],
   102→
   103→    # ── Wave 10: AC plan progress in Quick Look + CLI ────────────────────────
   104→    # Depends on: 025 (status --watch reloads plan), 026 (ac_spec_id in Meta), 028 (renderer)
   105→    ["027", "AC plan progress: Quick Look Plan tab + CLI block bars",   10, 3, "direct"],
   106→
   107→    # ── Wave 11: provider quota monitor + BopDeck feed ───────────────────────
   108→    # Research: Claude/Codex/Gemini OAuth APIs, Ollama /api/ps, opencode SSE.
   109→    # Sequential: 030 (trait + Claude) → 031 (Codex + Gemini) → 032 (Ollama + opencode) → 033 (watch + BopDeck)
   110→    ["030", "providers scaffold + Claude OAuth (5h/7d quota meters)",   11, 3, "direct"],
   111→    ["031", "providers: Codex OAuth+RPC + Gemini OAuth+quota API",      11, 4, "direct"],

   113→    ["033", "providers --watch + BopDeck socket feed + history JSONL",  11, 4, "direct"],
   114→
   115→    # ── Wave 7: lightweight UX + Zellij live links + security ────────────────
   116→    # Goals: working Zellij deep links in Quick Look, live log tail,
   117→    # color-coded CLI, serve.rs security hardening, event-driven dispatcher.
   118→    ["017", "Quick Look: Zellij live links + live log tail",              7, 4, "direct"],
   119→    ["018", "CLI UX: color-coded states + summary stats + better errors", 7, 2, "direct"],
   120→    ["019", "bop serve: security hardening (timing, auth, rate limit)",   7, 3, "direct"],
   121→    ["020", "event-driven dispatcher: replace polling with notify",       7, 3, "direct"],
   122→    ["021", "bop init: zero-config Zellij onboarding + bop doctor",      7, 2, "direct"],
   123→  ]
   124→}
   125→
   126→def cooldown [cost: int] {
   127→  match $cost {
   128→    1 => 20
   129→    2 => 40
   130→    3 => 75
   131→    4 => 120
   132→    _ => 60
   133→  }
   134→}
   135→
   136→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   137→# State helpers
   138→# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   139→
   140→def load_state [] {
   141→  if ($STATE_FILE | path exists) {
   142→    open $STATE_FILE
   143→  } else {
   144→    {completed: [], failed: [], skipped: []}
   145→  }
   146→}
   147→
   148→def save_state [state: record] {
   149→  $state | to json --indent 2 | save --force $STATE_FILE
   150→}

   125→private let featureLifecycleStageOrder: [(key: String, label: String)] = [

   130→]
   131→
   132→private func stageDisplayName(_ key: String) -> String {
   133→    switch key.lowercased() {
   134→    case "spec": return "Spec"
   135→    case "plan": return "Plan"
   136→    case "implement": return "Code"
   137→    case "qa": return "QA"
   138→    case "analyze": return "Analyze"
   139→    case "discover": return "Discover"
   140→    case "generate": return "Generate"
   141→    case "roadmap": return "Roadmap"
   142→    case "under_review": return "Under Review"
   143→    case "planned": return "Planned"
   144→    case "in_progress": return "In Progress"
   145→    case "done": return "Done"
   146→    default:
   147→        let lower = key.lowercased()
   148→        guard let first = lower.first else { return key }
   149→        return String(first).uppercased() + lower.dropFirst()
   150→    }

    23→/// Some fields (e.g. `glyph`, `token`, `stage`) are not consumed by every
    24→/// renderer but are part of the public data model for future consumers
    25→/// (spec 029 TUI, vibekanban provider).
    26→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]

    31→    /// SMP playing-card glyph (U+1F0xx) — may be absent on older cards.
    32→    pub glyph: Option<String>,
    33→    /// BMP-safe suit+rank token (e.g. "♠A") for terminals that lack SMP.
    34→    pub token: Option<String>,
    35→    /// Human-readable title (falls back to `id`).
    36→    pub title: String,
    37→    /// Current pipeline stage (e.g. "implement", "qa").
    38→    pub stage: String,
    39→    /// Priority rank: 1=Urgent .. 4=Low; None = unset.
    40→    pub priority: Option<i64>,
    41→    /// Overall completion 0–100.
    42→    pub progress: u8,
    43→    /// Provider from the most recent run (e.g. "claude", "codex").
    44→    pub provider: Option<String>,
    45→    /// Wall-clock seconds from the most recent run.
    46→    pub elapsed_s: Option<u64>,
    47→    /// AC plan phase name (from `implementation_plan.json`).
    48→    pub phase_name: Option<String>,

    52→    pub failure_reason: Option<String>,
    53→    /// Process exit code from the last run.
    54→    pub exit_code: Option<i32>,
    55→}

entries.content:
Planning is complete. Here's a summary of what was created:

## Planning Session Complete — Spec 027

### Files Created

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `context.json` | Investigation findings, file mappings, patterns observed |
| `implementation_plan.json` | 6 phases, 14 subtasks, dependency graph |
| `init.sh` | Build/test verification script |
| `build-progress.txt` | Progress tracking document |

### Implementation Plan Summary

**6 Phases, 14 Subtasks:**

1. **Core Data Model** (1 subtask) — Add `ac_spec_id: Option<String>` to `Meta` struct in bop-core
2. **Plan Loader Module** (3 subtasks) — New `acplan.rs` in bop-cli with `AcPlan` types, git root discovery, and `CardView` enrichment
3. **CLI Rendering** (4 subtasks) — Extend `CardView` with subtask counts, update all 5 renderers + kanban widget for N/T display + half-circle glyphs, wire enrichment into `bop list` and `--watch`
4. **Quick Look Plan Tab** (3 subtasks) — New `Plan` tab in Swift with SF Symbol icons, per-phase collapsible rows, lazy loading
5. **dispatch.nu Card Linkage** (1 subtask) — Write `ac_spec_id` when dispatching specs
6. **Verification + Output** (2 subtasks) — `make check` + `output/result.md`

### Key Architecture Insights

- `CardView` already has `phase_name`/`phase_frac` fields — they just need to be populated from plan data
- Half-circle glyphs (`○◔◑◕●`) use **different thresholds** than existing `moon_glyph` — new function needed
- All changes are **additive** — cards without `ac_spec_id` render identically to before
- Phase 4 (Swift) and Phase 5 (Nushell) can run **in parallel** with Phase 2→3 (Rust) since they touch different files
I'll start by reading the full Meta struct and understanding the existing patterns, particularly the `zellij_session` field pattern.

entries.detail:
1→# AC implementation plan progress in Quick Look + CLI
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→Auto-Claude's `implementation_plan.json` has per-phase subtask state
     6→(pending / in_progress / completed) that gives a clear picture of how far
     7→along a job is. Neither the Quick Look card preview nor `bop list` surface
     8→this — only raw `progress: Int` from `meta.json` is shown. The goal is to
     9→pipe AC's phase/subtask tree into the card's visual layer with zero extra
    10→tooling: block progress bars in the CLI, and a dedicated "Plan" tab in Quick
    11→Look alongside the existing Subtasks tab.
    12→
    13→## Data model
    14→
    15→### 1. `ac_spec_id` field in `Meta`
    16→
    17→Add to `crates/bop-core/src/lib.rs`:
    18→
    19→```rust
    20→pub ac_spec_id: Option<String>,   // e.g. "022"
    21→```
    22→
    23→This is the only link needed. Quick Look and CLI resolve the full path:
    24→
    25→```
    26→<git_root>/.auto-claude/specs/<ac_spec_id>-*/implementation_plan.json
    27→```
    28→
    29→Use a glob search (`read_dir` + prefix match on `<ac_spec_id>-`) to find the
    30→spec dir — do not hardcode the slug.
    31→
    32→### 2. How `ac_spec_id` gets written
    33→
    34→Two entry points:
    35→
    36→**dispatch.nu** — when `write_approval` fires before spawning a pane, also
    37→search `.cards/` (all state dirs) for a card whose `meta.json` `id` matches
    38→the spec slug or whose `title` contains the spec ID. If found, write
    39→`ac_spec_id` into that card's meta. This is the dogfood path where bop uses
    40→itself to track its own development.
    41→
    42→**Adapter convention** — if an adapter runs AC internally, it can write
    43→`ac_spec_id` to `meta.json` via `write_meta` before exiting.
    44→
    45→For cards with no AC spec, `ac_spec_id` is absent — no change to rendering.
    46→
    47→## CLI changes (`crates/bop-cli/src/main.rs` or status command)
    48→
    49→### `bop list` / `bop status` inline progress
    50→
    51→When a card has `ac_spec_id` set, load `implementation_plan.json` and render
    52→a compact one-liner after the card row:
    53→
    54→```
    55→⚙ 022-atomic-write-meta   running   claude   6m 14s
    56→  ████████████░░░░  6/7   Phase 2: CLI + Docs  ◑
    57→```
    58→
    59→Format:
    60→- Block bar: 16 chars, `█` per completed subtask fraction, `░` remainder
    61→- `N/T` count: completed subtasks / total subtasks across all phases
    62→- Current phase name (truncated to 24 chars)
    63→- Phase completion half-circle: `○◔◑◕●` (0% 25% 50% 75% 100%)
    64→  - U+25CB, U+25D4, U+25D1, U+25D5, U+25CF — all BMP-safe
    65→
    66→Half-circle thresholds (fraction of subtasks in current phase done):
    67→- 0 % → `○`
    68→- 1–33 % → `◔`
    69→- 34–66 % → `◑`
    70→- 67–99 % → `◕`
    71→- 100 % → `●`
    72→
    73→Colors: completed subtasks = green, in_progress = amber, pending = dim.
    74→Only shown for cards where `ac_spec_id` resolves to a readable file.
    75→
    76→### `bop status --watch` (spec 025)
    77→
    78→When the plan view is live, also reload `implementation_plan.json` on each
    79→notify event — it changes as the AC agent commits subtasks.
    80→
    81→## Quick Look changes (`macos/bop/PreviewViewController.swift`)
    82→
    83→### New tab: "Plan"
    84→
    85→Add `plan = "Plan"` to the `CardSection` enum (alongside `subtasks`).
    86→Show the tab only if `ac_spec_id` is set and `implementation_plan.json` loads.
    87→
    88→The Plan tab reads `implementation_plan.json` from the git root (walk up from
    89→the card dir via `../` until `.git` or `.auto-claude` is found).
    90→
    91→### New Codable structs
    92→
    93→```swift
    94→private struct AcPlan: Codable {
    95→    let phases: [AcPhase]
    96→}
    97→
    98→private struct AcPhase: Codable {
    99→    let id: String
   100→    let name: String
   101→    let subtasks: [AcSubtask]
   102→}
   103→
   104→private struct AcSubtask: Codable {
   105→    let id: String
   106→    let description: String
   107→    let status: String   // "pending" | "in_progress" | "completed"
   108→}
   109→```
   110→
   111→### Plan tab rendering
   112→
   113→Overall progress bar at top (same style as existing `subtasksTab` header):
   114→
   115→```
   116→8 of 12 subtasks complete          67%
   117→████████████░░░░░░░░
   118→```
   119→
   120→Then per-phase collapsible rows:
   121→
   122→```
   123→◉  Phase 1 — Crash Safety          ●●●●●  5/5
   124→   ✓ Make write_meta atomic
   125→   ✓ Make write_providers atomic
   126→   ✓ Add recover_orphans function
   127→   ✓ Call recover_orphans on startup
   128→   ✓ Add bop recover CLI command
   129→
   130→◔  Phase 2 — CLI + Docs            ●○○    1/3
   131→   ✓ Write result.md (in_progress)
   132→   ○ Run make check
   133→   ○ Final review
   134→```
   135→
   136→Glyph legend (BMP-safe, existing SF Symbols for Quick Look):
   137→- Phase status icon: `circle.fill` (done), `circle.lefthalf.filled` (partial),
   138→  `circle` (not started) — SF Symbols, no font dep
   139→- Subtask row: `checkmark.circle.fill` (completed, green),
   140→  `arrow.triangle.2.circlepath` (in_progress, amber), `circle` (pending, dim)
   141→  — matches the existing `subtasksTab` icon style
   142→
   143→Phase name row background: match existing card row style
   144→(`Color.black.opacity(0.15)` + `RoundedRectangle` border).
   145→
   146→### `BopCardMeta` addition
   147→
   148→```swift
   149→let acSpecId: String?
   150→
   151→enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
   152→    // ... existing keys ...
   153→    case acSpecId = "ac_spec_id"
   154→}
   155→```
   156→
   157→Load `AcPlan` lazily when the Plan tab is first shown — do not block the
   158→initial card render. Use a `@State var acPlan: AcPlan? = nil` loaded in
   159→`.task { }` on the tab view.
   160→
   161→Git root discovery: walk parent dirs from `cardURL.deletingLastPathComponent()`
   162→until a dir containing `.auto-claude` is found, max 6 levels. Cache the result.
   163→
   164→## Acceptance
   165→
   166→- `Meta` has `ac_spec_id: Option<String>` field (serde rename `ac_spec_id`)
   167→- `bop list` renders block bar + phase half-circle for cards with `ac_spec_id`
   168→- `bop status --watch` reloads plan on file changes
   169→- Quick Look shows "Plan" tab when `ac_spec_id` resolves to a readable plan
   170→- Plan tab shows overall block bar + per-phase subtask list with SF Symbol icons
   171→- Git root discovery walks up from card dir (max 6 levels)
   172→- `make check` passes
   173→- `output/result.md` with ASCII screenshot of CLI output and description of QL tab
   174→

entries.content: Now update the subtask status in the implementation plan:

Subtask 1-1 is complete. Here's what was done:

1. **Added `ac_spec_id` field to `Meta` struct** in `crates/bop-core/src/lib.rs`:
   - `pub ac_spec_id: Option<String>` with `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`
   - Placed after `zellij_pane` in a new `// ── Auto-Claude linkage ──` section
   - Added doc comment explaining the field links to AC implementation plans

2. **Added to `Default` impl**: `ac_spec_id: None`

3. **Two round-trip tests**:
   - `meta_ac_spec_id_round_trips` — verifies the field serializes as `"ac_spec_id"` in JSON and round-trips through write/read
   - `meta_ac_spec_id_omitted_when_none` — verifies the field is omitted from JSON when `None`

4. **Verification**: All 106 bop-core tests pass, including the 2 new ones.
I'll start by reading the necessary files to understand the patterns and current state.

    35→
    36→**dispatch.nu** — when `write_approval` fires before spawning a pane, also
    37→search `.cards/` (all state dirs) for a card whose `meta.json` `id` matches
    38→the spec slug or whose `title` contains the spec ID. If found, write
    39→`ac_spec_id` into that card's meta. This is the dogfood path where bop uses
    40→itself to track its own development.
    41→
    42→**Adapter convention** — if an adapter runs AC internally, it can write
    43→`ac_spec_id` to `meta.json` via `write_meta` before exiting.
    44→
    45→For cards with no AC spec, `ac_spec_id` is absent — no change to rendering.
    46→
    47→## CLI changes (`crates/bop-cli/src/main.rs` or status command)
    48→
    49→### `bop list` / `bop status` inline progress
    50→
    51→When a card has `ac_spec_id` set, load `implementation_plan.json` and render
    52→a compact one-liner after the card row:
    53→
    54→```
    55→⚙ 022-atomic-write-meta   running   claude   6m 14s
    56→  ████████████░░░░  6/7   Phase 2: CLI + Docs  ◑
    57→```
    58→
    59→Format:
    60→- Block bar: 16 chars, `█` per completed subtask fraction, `░` remainder
    61→- `N/T` count: completed subtasks / total subtasks across all phases
    62→- Current phase name (truncated to 24 chars)
    63→- Phase completion half-circle: `○◔◑◕●` (0% 25% 50% 75% 100%)
    64→  - U+25CB, U+25D4, U+25D1, U+25D5, U+25CF — all BMP-safe
    65→
    66→Half-circle thresholds (fraction of subtasks in current phase done):
    67→- 0 % → `○`
    68→- 1–33 % → `◔`
    69→- 34–66 % → `◑`
    70→- 67–99 % → `◕`
    71→- 100 % → `●`
    72→
    73→Colors: completed subtasks = green, in_progress = amber, pending = dim.
    74→Only shown for cards where `ac_spec_id` resolves to a readable file.
    75→
    76→### `bop status --watch` (spec 025)
    77→
    78→When the plan view is live, also reload `implementation_plan.json` on each
    79→notify event — it changes as the AC agent commits subtasks.
    80→
    81→## Quick Look changes (`macos/bop/PreviewViewController.swift`)
    82→
    83→### New tab: "Plan"
    84→
    85→Add `plan = "Plan"` to the `CardSection` enum (alongside `subtasks`).
    86→Show the tab only if `ac_spec_id` is set and `implementation_plan.json` loads.
    87→
    88→The Plan tab reads `implementation_plan.json` from the git root (walk up from
    89→the card dir via `../` until `.git` or `.auto-claude` is found).
    90→
    91→### New Codable structs
    92→
    93→```swift
    94→private struct AcPlan: Codable {
    95→    let phases: [AcPhase]
    96→}
    97→
    98→private struct AcPhase: Codable {
    99→    let id: String
   100→    let name: String
   101→    let subtasks: [AcSubtask]
   102→}
   103→
   104→private struct AcSubtask: Codable {
   105→    let id: String
   106→    let description: String
   107→    let status: String   // "pending" | "in_progress" | "completed"
   108→}
   109→```
   110→
   111→### Plan tab rendering
   112→
   113→Overall progress bar at top (same style as existing `subtasksTab` header):
   114→
   115→```
   116→8 of 12 subtasks complete          67%
   117→████████████░░░░░░░░
   118→```
   119→
   120→Then per-phase collapsible rows:
   121→
   122→```
   123→◉  Phase 1 — Crash Safety          ●●●●●  5/5
   124→   ✓ Make write_meta atomic
   125→   ✓ Make write_providers atomic
   126→   ✓ Add recover_orphans function
   127→   ✓ Call recover_orphans on startup
   128→   ✓ Add bop recover CLI command
   129→
   130→◔  Phase 2 — CLI + Docs            ●○○    1/3
   131→   ✓ Write result.md (in_progress)
   132→   ○ Run make check
   133→   ○ Final review
   134→```
   135→
   136→Glyph legend (BMP-safe, existing SF Symbols for Quick Look):
   137→- Phase status icon: `circle.fill` (done), `circle.lefthalf.filled` (partial),
   138→  `circle` (not started) — SF Symbols, no font dep
   139→- Subtask row: `checkmark.circle.fill` (completed, green),
   140→  `arrow.triangle.2.circlepath` (in_progress, amber), `circle` (pending, dim)
   141→  — matches the existing `subtasksTab` icon style
   142→
   143→Phase name row background: match existing card row style
   144→(`Color.black.opacity(0.15)` + `RoundedRectangle` border).
   145→
   146→### `BopCardMeta` addition
   147→
   148→```swift
   149→let acSpecId: String?
   150→
   151→enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
   152→    // ... existing keys ...
   153→    case acSpecId = "ac_spec_id"
   154→}
   155→```
   156→
   157→Load `AcPlan` lazily when the Plan tab is first shown — do not block the
   158→initial card render. Use a `@State var acPlan: AcPlan? = nil` loaded in
   159→`.task { }` on the tab view.
   160→
   161→Git root discovery: walk parent dirs from `cardURL.deletingLastPathComponent()`
   162→until a dir containing `.auto-claude` is found, max 6 levels. Cache the result.
   163→
   164→## Acceptance
   165→
   166→- `Meta` has `ac_spec_id: Option<String>` field (serde rename `ac_spec_id`)
   167→- `bop list` renders block bar + phase half-circle for cards with `ac_spec_id`
   168→- `bop status --watch` reloads plan on file changes
   169→- Quick Look shows "Plan" tab when `ac_spec_id` resolves to a readable plan
   170→- Plan tab shows overall block bar + per-phase subtask list with SF Symbol icons
   171→- Git root discovery walks up from card dir (max 6 levels)
   172→- `make check` passes
   173→- `output/result.md` with ASCII screenshot of CLI output and description of QL tab
   174→

entries.content: Now update the subtask status in the implementation plan:

Subtask 2-1 is complete. Here's what was done:

**Created `crates/bop-cli/src/acplan.rs`** with:
- **`AcPlan`** struct — top-level plan with `phases: Vec<AcPhase>` (serde `Deserialize`)
- **`AcPhase`** struct — phase with `id`, `name`, `subtasks: Vec<AcSubtask>`
- **`AcSubtask`** struct — subtask with `id`, `description`, `status` (defaults to `"pending"`)
- **`parse_plan(path)`** — reads and parses `implementation_plan.json`, handles missing/invalid files with anyhow context
- **`half_circle_glyph(frac)`** — returns BMP-safe circle glyphs per spec thresholds: `○` (0%), `◔` (1-33%), `◑` (34-66%), `◕` (67-99%), `●` (100%)
- **18 unit tests** covering all glyph thresholds, boundary values, edge cases (negative, >1.0), plan parsing, extra field tolerance, default status, missing/invalid files, and BMP safety

**Modified `crates/bop-cli/src/main.rs`** — added `mod acplan;` declaration

**Fixed `crates/bop-cli/src/cards.rs`** — added missing `ac_spec_id: None` to 10 `Meta` struct initializers that were left over from subtask-1-1

entries.content: Now update the subtask status in the implementation plan:

Subtask 2-2 is complete. Here's what was implemented:

**`find_git_root(start_dir)`** — Walks up to 6 parent directories from `start_dir`, looking for `.git` or `.auto-claude` directory markers. Returns `Option<PathBuf>` of the project root. Uses pure filesystem checks (no `git` subprocess needed), so it works in minimal environments and test temp-dirs.

**`resolve_spec_dir(git_root, ac_spec_id)`** — Reads `<git_root>/.auto-claude/specs/` and finds the first directory matching the `<ac_spec_id>-*` prefix pattern. Returns `Option<PathBuf>` pointing to the `implementation_plan.json` file inside that directory, or `None` if not found.

**9 tests added**, all passing:
- 5 tests for `find_git_root` (`.git` detection, `.auto-claude` detection, parent walking, plain dir returns None, max depth enforcement)
- 4 tests for `resolve_spec_dir` (matching spec found, no plan file, unknown ID, no specs dir)
I'll start by reading the necessary files to understand the patterns and current implementation.

entries.detail:
1→# AC implementation plan progress in Quick Look + CLI
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→Auto-Claude's `implementation_plan.json` has per-phase subtask state
     6→(pending / in_progress / completed) that gives a clear picture of how far
     7→along a job is. Neither the Quick Look card preview nor `bop list` surface
     8→this — only raw `progress: Int` from `meta.json` is shown. The goal is to
     9→pipe AC's phase/subtask tree into the card's visual layer with zero extra
    10→tooling: block progress bars in the CLI, and a dedicated "Plan" tab in Quick
    11→Look alongside the existing Subtasks tab.
    12→
    13→## Data model
    14→
    15→### 1. `ac_spec_id` field in `Meta`
    16→
    17→Add to `crates/bop-core/src/lib.rs`:
    18→
    19→```rust
    20→pub ac_spec_id: Option<String>,   // e.g. "022"
    21→```
    22→
    23→This is the only link needed. Quick Look and CLI resolve the full path:
    24→
    25→```
    26→<git_root>/.auto-claude/specs/<ac_spec_id>-*/implementation_plan.json
    27→```
    28→
    29→Use a glob search (`read_dir` + prefix match on `<ac_spec_id>-`) to find the
    30→spec dir — do not hardcode the slug.
    31→
    32→### 2. How `ac_spec_id` gets written
    33→
    34→Two entry points:
    35→
    36→**dispatch.nu** — when `write_approval` fires before spawning a pane, also
    37→search `.cards/` (all state dirs) for a card whose `meta.json` `id` matches
    38→the spec slug or whose `title` contains the spec ID. If found, write
    39→`ac_spec_id` into that card's meta. This is the dogfood path where bop uses
    40→itself to track its own development.
    41→
    42→**Adapter convention** — if an adapter runs AC internally, it can write
    43→`ac_spec_id` to `meta.json` via `write_meta` before exiting.
    44→
    45→For cards with no AC spec, `ac_spec_id` is absent — no change to rendering.
    46→
    47→## CLI changes (`crates/bop-cli/src/main.rs` or status command)
    48→
    49→### `bop list` / `bop status` inline progress
    50→
    51→When a card has `ac_spec_id` set, load `implementation_plan.json` and render
    52→a compact one-liner after the card row:
    53→
    54→```
    55→⚙ 022-atomic-write-meta   running   claude   6m 14s
    56→  ████████████░░░░  6/7   Phase 2: CLI + Docs  ◑
    57→```
    58→
    59→Format:
    60→- Block bar: 16 chars, `█` per completed subtask fraction, `░` remainder
    61→- `N/T` count: completed subtasks / total subtasks across all phases
    62→- Current phase name (truncated to 24 chars)
    63→- Phase completion half-circle: `○◔◑◕●` (0% 25% 50% 75% 100%)
    64→  - U+25CB, U+25D4, U+25D1, U+25D5, U+25CF — all BMP-safe
    65→
    66→Half-circle thresholds (fraction of subtasks in current phase done):
    67→- 0 % → `○`
    68→- 1–33 % → `◔`
    69→- 34–66 % → `◑`
    70→- 67–99 % → `◕`
    71→- 100 % → `●`
    72→
    73→Colors: completed subtasks = green, in_progress = amber, pending = dim.
    74→Only shown for cards where `ac_spec_id` resolves to a readable file.
    75→
    76→### `bop status --watch` (spec 025)
    77→
    78→When the plan view is live, also reload `implementation_plan.json` on each
    79→notify event — it changes as the AC agent commits subtasks.
    80→
    81→## Quick Look changes (`macos/bop/PreviewViewController.swift`)
    82→
    83→### New tab: "Plan"
    84→
    85→Add `plan = "Plan"` to the `CardSection` enum (alongside `subtasks`).
    86→Show the tab only if `ac_spec_id` is set and `implementation_plan.json` loads.
    87→
    88→The Plan tab reads `implementation_plan.json` from the git root (walk up from
    89→the card dir via `../` until `.git` or `.auto-claude` is found).
    90→
    91→### New Codable structs
    92→
    93→```swift
    94→private struct AcPlan: Codable {
    95→    let phases: [AcPhase]
    96→}
    97→
    98→private struct AcPhase: Codable {
    99→    let id: String
   100→    let name: String
   101→    let subtasks: [AcSubtask]
   102→}
   103→
   104→private struct AcSubtask: Codable {
   105→    let id: String
   106→    let description: String
   107→    let status: String   // "pending" | "in_progress" | "completed"
   108→}
   109→```
   110→
   111→### Plan tab rendering
   112→
   113→Overall progress bar at top (same style as existing `subtasksTab` header):
   114→
   115→```
   116→8 of 12 subtasks complete          67%
   117→████████████░░░░░░░░
   118→```
   119→
   120→Then per-phase collapsible rows:
   121→
   122→```
   123→◉  Phase 1 — Crash Safety          ●●●●●  5/5
   124→   ✓ Make write_meta atomic
   125→   ✓ Make write_providers atomic
   126→   ✓ Add recover_orphans function
   127→   ✓ Call recover_orphans on startup
   128→   ✓ Add bop recover CLI command
   129→
   130→◔  Phase 2 — CLI + Docs            ●○○    1/3
   131→   ✓ Write result.md (in_progress)
   132→   ○ Run make check
   133→   ○ Final review
   134→```
   135→
   136→Glyph legend (BMP-safe, existing SF Symbols for Quick Look):
   137→- Phase status icon: `circle.fill` (done), `circle.lefthalf.filled` (partial),
   138→  `circle` (not started) — SF Symbols, no font dep
   139→- Subtask row: `checkmark.circle.fill` (completed, green),
   140→  `arrow.triangle.2.circlepath` (in_progress, amber), `circle` (pending, dim)
   141→  — matches the existing `subtasksTab` icon style
   142→
   143→Phase name row background: match existing card row style
   144→(`Color.black.opacity(0.15)` + `RoundedRectangle` border).
   145→
   146→### `BopCardMeta` addition
   147→
   148→```swift
   149→let acSpecId: String?
   150→
   151→enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
   152→    // ... existing keys ...
   153→    case acSpecId = "ac_spec_id"
   154→}
   155→```
   156→
   157→Load `AcPlan` lazily when the Plan tab is first shown — do not block the
   158→initial card render. Use a `@State var acPlan: AcPlan? = nil` loaded in
   159→`.task { }` on the tab view.
   160→
   161→Git root discovery: walk parent dirs from `cardURL.deletingLastPathComponent()`
   162→until a dir containing `.auto-claude` is found, max 6 levels. Cache the result.
   163→
   164→## Acceptance
   165→
   166→- `Meta` has `ac_spec_id: Option<String>` field (serde rename `ac_spec_id`)
   167→- `bop list` renders block bar + phase half-circle for cards with `ac_spec_id`
   168→- `bop status --watch` reloads plan on file changes
   169→- Quick Look shows "Plan" tab when `ac_spec_id` resolves to a readable plan
   170→- Plan tab shows overall block bar + per-phase subtask list with SF Symbol icons
   171→- Git root discovery walks up from card dir (max 6 levels)
   172→- `make check` passes
   173→- `output/result.md` with ASCII screenshot of CLI output and description of QL tab
   174→

entries.detail:
1→# AC implementation plan progress in Quick Look + CLI
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→Auto-Claude's `implementation_plan.json` has per-phase subtask state
     6→(pending / in_progress / completed) that gives a clear picture of how far
     7→along a job is. Neither the Quick Look card preview nor `bop list` surface
     8→this — only raw `progress: Int` from `meta.json` is shown. The goal is to
     9→pipe AC's phase/subtask tree into the card's visual layer with zero extra
    10→tooling: block progress bars in the CLI, and a dedicated "Plan" tab in Quick
    11→Look alongside the existing Subtasks tab.
    12→
    13→## Data model
    14→
    15→### 1. `ac_spec_id` field in `Meta`
    16→
    17→Add to `crates/bop-core/src/lib.rs`:
    18→
    19→```rust
    20→pub ac_spec_id: Option<String>,   // e.g. "022"
    21→```
    22→
    23→This is the only link needed. Quick Look and CLI resolve the full path:
    24→
    25→```
    26→<git_root>/.auto-claude/specs/<ac_spec_id>-*/implementation_plan.json
    27→```
    28→
    29→Use a glob search (`read_dir` + prefix match on `<ac_spec_id>-`) to find the
    30→spec dir — do not hardcode the slug.
    31→
    32→### 2. How `ac_spec_id` gets written
    33→
    34→Two entry points:
    35→
    36→**dispatch.nu** — when `write_approval` fires before spawning a pane, also
    37→search `.cards/` (all state dirs) for a card whose `meta.json` `id` matches
    38→the spec slug or whose `title` contains the spec ID. If found, write
    39→`ac_spec_id` into that card's meta. This is the dogfood path where bop uses
    40→itself to track its own development.
    41→
    42→**Adapter convention** — if an adapter runs AC internally, it can write
    43→`ac_spec_id` to `meta.json` via `write_meta` before exiting.
    44→
    45→For cards with no AC spec, `ac_spec_id` is absent — no change to rendering.
    46→
    47→## CLI changes (`crates/bop-cli/src/main.rs` or status command)
    48→
    49→### `bop list` / `bop status` inline progress
    50→
    51→When a card has `ac_spec_id` set, load `implementation_plan.json` and render
    52→a compact one-liner after the card row:
    53→
    54→```
    55→⚙ 022-atomic-write-meta   running   claude   6m 14s
    56→  ████████████░░░░  6/7   Phase 2: CLI + Docs  ◑
    57→```
    58→
    59→Format:
    60→- Block bar: 16 chars, `█` per completed subtask fraction, `░` remainder
    61→- `N/T` count: completed subtasks / total subtasks across all phases
    62→- Current phase name (truncated to 24 chars)
    63→- Phase completion half-circle: `○◔◑◕●` (0% 25% 50% 75% 100%)
    64→  - U+25CB, U+25D4, U+25D1, U+25D5, U+25CF — all BMP-safe
    65→
    66→Half-circle thresholds (fraction of subtasks in current phase done):
    67→- 0 % → `○`
    68→- 1–33 % → `◔`
    69→- 34–66 % → `◑`
    70→- 67–99 % → `◕`
    71→- 100 % → `●`
    72→
    73→Colors: completed subtasks = green, in_progress = amber, pending = dim.
    74→Only shown for cards where `ac_spec_id` resolves to a readable file.
    75→
    76→### `bop status --watch` (spec 025)
    77→
    78→When the plan view is live, also reload `implementation_plan.json` on each
    79→notify event — it changes as the AC agent commits subtasks.
    80→
    81→## Quick Look changes (`macos/bop/PreviewViewController.swift`)
    82→
    83→### New tab: "Plan"
    84→
    85→Add `plan = "Plan"` to the `CardSection` enum (alongside `subtasks`).
    86→Show the tab only if `ac_spec_id` is set and `implementation_plan.json` loads.
    87→
    88→The Plan tab reads `implementation_plan.json` from the git root (walk up from
    89→the card dir via `../` until `.git` or `.auto-claude` is found).
    90→
    91→### New Codable structs
    92→
    93→```swift
    94→private struct AcPlan: Codable {
    95→    let phases: [AcPhase]
    96→}
    97→
    98→private struct AcPhase: Codable {
    99→    let id: String
   100→    let name: String
   101→    let subtasks: [AcSubtask]
   102→}
   103→
   104→private struct AcSubtask: Codable {
   105→    let id: String
   106→    let description: String
   107→    let status: String   // "pending" | "in_progress" | "completed"
   108→}
   109→```
   110→
   111→### Plan tab rendering
   112→
   113→Overall progress bar at top (same style as existing `subtasksTab` header):
   114→
   115→```
   116→8 of 12 subtasks complete          67%
   117→████████████░░░░░░░░
   118→```
   119→
   120→Then per-phase collapsible rows:
   121→
   122→```
   123→◉  Phase 1 — Crash Safety          ●●●●●  5/5
   124→   ✓ Make write_meta atomic
   125→   ✓ Make write_providers atomic
   126→   ✓ Add recover_orphans function
   127→   ✓ Call recover_orphans on startup
   128→   ✓ Add bop recover CLI command
   129→
   130→◔  Phase 2 — CLI + Docs            ●○○    1/3
   131→   ✓ Write result.md (in_progress)
   132→   ○ Run make check
   133→   ○ Final review
   134→```
   135→
   136→Glyph legend (BMP-safe, existing SF Symbols for Quick Look):
   137→- Phase status icon: `circle.fill` (done), `circle.lefthalf.filled` (partial),
   138→  `circle` (not started) — SF Symbols, no font dep
   139→- Subtask row: `checkmark.circle.fill` (completed, green),
   140→  `arrow.triangle.2.circlepath` (in_progress, amber), `circle` (pending, dim)
   141→  — matches the existing `subtasksTab` icon style
   142→
   143→Phase name row background: match existing card row style
   144→(`Color.black.opacity(0.15)` + `RoundedRectangle` border).
   145→
   146→### `BopCardMeta` addition
   147→
   148→```swift
   149→let acSpecId: String?
   150→
   151→enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
   152→    // ... existing keys ...
   153→    case acSpecId = "ac_spec_id"
   154→}
   155→```
   156→
   157→Load `AcPlan` lazily when the Plan tab is first shown — do not block the
   158→initial card render. Use a `@State var acPlan: AcPlan? = nil` loaded in
   159→`.task { }` on the tab view.
   160→
   161→Git root discovery: walk parent dirs from `cardURL.deletingLastPathComponent()`
   162→until a dir containing `.auto-claude` is found, max 6 levels. Cache the result.
   163→
   164→## Acceptance
   165→
   166→- `Meta` has `ac_spec_id: Option<String>` field (serde rename `ac_spec_id`)
   167→- `bop list` renders block bar + phase half-circle for cards with `ac_spec_id`
   168→- `bop status --watch` reloads plan on file changes
   169→- Quick Look shows "Plan" tab when `ac_spec_id` resolves to a readable plan
   170→- Plan tab shows overall block bar + per-phase subtask list with SF Symbol icons
   171→- Git root discovery walks up from card dir (max 6 levels)
   172→- `make check` passes
   173→- `output/result.md` with ASCII screenshot of CLI output and description of QL tab
   174→

entries.content:
All 36 acplan tests pass. The code for subtask 2-3 is already fully implemented:

1. **`PlanSummary` struct** - with `completed`, `total`, `current_phase_name`, `current_phase_frac`
2. **`plan_summary()`** - correctly identifies current phase, counts completed/total, handles edge cases
3. **`enrich_card_view()`** - checks `ac_spec_id`, resolves spec dir, loads plan, populates `phase_name`, `phase_frac`, `progress`
4. **Tests** - 6 plan_summary tests + 3 enrich_card_view tests all passing

The `ac_subtasks_done/ac_subtasks_total` fields are explicitly deferred to subtask 3-1 which adds them to `CardView` in `render/mod.rs`.

Let me check if there are any uncommitted changes, and then mark the subtask as completed.

This means the changes were already committed in a previous session (likely included in one of the existing subtask commits). The code and tests are all in place and passing. Let me now update the implementation plan to mark subtask-2-3 as completed.

Subtask 2-3 is now marked as **completed**. Here's a summary:

### ✅ Subtask 2-3 — Complete

**What was implemented:**

1. **`PlanSummary` struct** — Aggregate statistics with `completed`, `total`, `current_phase_name`, `current_phase_frac` fields

2. **`plan_summary(plan: &AcPlan) -> PlanSummary`** — Walks all phases to:
   - Count completed/total subtasks across all phases
   - Identify current phase (first with incomplete subtasks)
   - Calculate phase completion fraction
   - Handle edge cases: empty plan, all completed (reports last phase at 100%), phases with empty subtask lists

3. **`enrich_card_view(view: &mut CardView, meta: &Meta, git_root: &Path)`** — Enriches a CardView by:
   - Checking `meta.ac_spec_id` (no-op if `None`)
   - Resolving spec directory via `resolve_spec_dir`
   - Loading and parsing the plan
   - Populating `phase_name`, `phase_frac`, and `progress` on the view
   - Silently no-ops on any resolution/parse failure

4. **Tests** — 6 plan_summary tests + 3 enrich_card_view tests (36 total acplan tests passing):
   - `plan_summary_basic`, `plan_summary_all_completed`, `plan_summary_empty_plan`
   - `plan_summary_single_phase_none_done`, `plan_summary_skips_completed_phases`, `plan_summary_phase_with_empty_subtasks`
   - `enrich_card_view_populates_fields`, `enrich_card_view_noop_without_spec_id`, `enrich_card_view_noop_when_plan_missing`

entries.detail:
1→/// Detail overlay widget — card information panel on right 50% of body.
     2→///
     3→/// Renders on top of the kanban board using ratatui `Clear` + bordered
     4→/// `Block`. Shows: card id, provider, elapsed time, progress bar,
     5→/// phase/subtask tree from AC plan (`implementation_plan.json` in the
     6→/// card directory, if present). Scrollable with j/k.
     7→///
     8→/// Footer keybinds (rendered by the footer widget, not here):
     9→/// `[F3]logs [p]pause [r]retry [Esc]close`
    10→use std::fs;
    11→use std::path::Path;
    12→
    13→use ratatui::layout::Rect;
    14→use ratatui::style::{Color, Modifier, Style};
    15→use ratatui::text::{Line, Span};
    16→use ratatui::widgets::{Block, Clear, Paragraph, Wrap};
    17→use ratatui::Frame;
    18→
    19→use crate::render::CardView;
    20→
    21→// ── AC plan types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    22→
    23→/// A subtask entry from an AC `implementation_plan.json`.
    24→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    25→struct PlanSubtask {
    26→    id: String,
    27→    description: String,
    28→    status: String,
    29→}
    30→
    31→/// A phase entry from an AC `implementation_plan.json`.
    32→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    33→struct PlanPhase {
    34→    name: String,
    35→    subtasks: Vec<PlanSubtask>,
    36→}
    37→
    38→/// Parsed AC plan data (phases + subtasks).
    39→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    40→struct AcPlan {
    41→    phases: Vec<PlanPhase>,
    42→}
    43→
    44→// ── Plan parser ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    45→
    46→/// Read and parse `implementation_plan.json` from a card directory.
    47→///
    48→/// Returns `None` if the file doesn't exist or can't be parsed.
    49→/// Tolerant of missing fields — extracts what it can.
    50→fn read_ac_plan(card_dir: &Path) -> Option<AcPlan> {
    51→    let plan_path = card_dir.join("implementation_plan.json");
    52→    let content = fs::read_to_string(&plan_path).ok()?;
    53→    let root: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&content).ok()?;
    54→
    55→    let phases_arr = root.get("phases")?.as_array()?;
    56→    let mut phases = Vec::new();
    57→
    58→    for phase_val in phases_arr {
    59→        let name = phase_val
    60→            .get("name")
    61→            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
    62→            .unwrap_or("unnamed")
    63→            .to_string();
    64→
    65→        let mut subtasks = Vec::new();
    66→        if let Some(subs) = phase_val.get("subtasks").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {
    67→            for sub in subs {
    68→                let id = sub
    69→                    .get("id")
    70→                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
    71→                    .unwrap_or("")
    72→                    .to_string();
    73→                let description = sub
    74→                    .get("description")
    75→                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
    76→                    .unwrap_or("")
    77→                    .to_string();
    78→                let status = sub
    79→                    .get("status")
    80→                    .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
    81→                    .unwrap_or("pending")
    82→                    .to_string();
    83→
    84→                subtasks.push(PlanSubtask {
    85→                    id,
    86→                    description,
    87→                    status,
    88→                });
    89→            }
    90→        }
    91→
    92→        phases.push(PlanPhase { name, subtasks });
    93→    }
    94→
    95→    Some(AcPlan { phases })
    96→}
    97→
    98→// ── Rendering helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    99→
   100→/// Status glyph for a subtask status string.
   101→fn status_glyph(status: &str) -> &'static str {
   102→    match status {
   103→        "completed" => "✓",
   104→        "in_progress" => "▶",
   105→        "failed" => "✗",
   106→        _ => "·", // pending or unknown
   107→    }
   108→}
   109→
   110→/// Status color for a subtask status string.
   111→fn status_color(status: &str) -> Color {
   112→    match status {
   113→        "completed" => Color::Rgb(0x1E, 0x8A, 0x45),   // green
   114→        "in_progress" => Color::Rgb(0xB8, 0x69, 0x0F), // amber
   115→        "failed" => Color::Rgb(0xC4, 0x30, 0x30),      // red
   116→        _ => Color::DarkGray,                          // pending
   117→    }
   118→}
   119→
   120→/// Format seconds into human-readable "XmYYs" or "Xs".
   121→fn format_duration(secs: u64) -> String {
   122→    if secs >= 3600 {
   123→        format!("{}h{:02}m", secs / 3600, (secs % 3600) / 60)
   124→    } else if secs >= 60 {
   125→        format!("{}m{:02}s", secs / 60, secs % 60)
   126→    } else {
   127→        format!("{}s", secs)
   128→    }
   129→}
   130→
   131→/// Build the progress bar line.
   132→///
   133→/// Format: `████████░░░░░░░░░░░░ 42%`
   134→fn build_progress_bar(pct: u8, width: usize) -> Line<'static> {
   135→    let bar_width = width.saturating_sub(6).min(30); // reserve space for " NNN%"
   136→    let filled = (pct as usize * bar_width) / 100;
   137→    let empty = bar_width.saturating_sub(filled);
   138→
   139→    let bar = format!("{}{}", "█".repeat(filled), "░".repeat(empty));
   140→
   141→    let color = if pct >= 75 {
   142→        Color::Rgb(0x1E, 0x8A, 0x45) // green
   143→    } else if pct >= 40 {
   144→        Color::Rgb(0xB8, 0x69, 0x0F) // amber
   145→    } else {
   146→        Color::Rgb(0x3A, 0x5A, 0x8A) // blue
   147→    };
   148→
   149→    Line::from(vec![
   150→        Span::styled(bar, Style::default().fg(color)),
   151→        Span::styled(format!(" {:>3}%", pct), Style::default().fg(Color::White)),
   152→    ])
   153→}
   154→
   155→/// Build all content lines for the detail overlay.
   156→///
   157→/// Returns a `Vec<Line>` that can be scrolled via `detail_scroll`.
   158→fn build_detail_lines(card: &CardView, card_dir: Option<&Path>) -> Vec<Line<'static>> {
   159→    let mut lines: Vec<Line<'static>> = Vec::new();
   160→
   161→    // ── Card ID ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   162→    lines.push(Line::from(vec![
   163→        Span::styled(
   164→            "Card: ",
   165→            Style::default()
   166→                .fg(Color::DarkGray)
   167→                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
   168→        ),
   169→        Span::styled(card.id.clone(), Style::default().fg(Color::White)),
   170→    ]));
   171→
   172→    // ── State ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   173→    let state_color = match card.state.as_str() {
   174→        "pending" => Color::Rgb(0x3A, 0x5A, 0x8A),
   175→        "running" => Color::Rgb(0xB8, 0x69, 0x0F),
   176→        "done" => Color::Rgb(0x1E, 0x8A, 0x45),
   177→        "failed" => Color::Rgb(0xC4, 0x30, 0x30),
   178→        "merged" => Color::Rgb(0x6B, 0x3D, 0xB8),
   179→        _ => Color::Gray,
   180→    };
   181→    lines.push(Line::from(vec![
   182→        Span::styled(
   183→            "State: ",
   184→            Style::default()
   185→                .fg(Color::DarkGray)
   186→                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
   187→        ),
   188→        Span::styled(card.state.clone(), Style::default().fg(state_color)),
   189→    ]));
   190→
   191→    // ── Stage ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   192→    lines.push(Line::from(vec![
   193→        Span::styled(
   194→            "Stage: ",
   195→            Style::default()
   196→                .fg(Color::DarkGray)
   197→                .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
   198→        ),
   199→        Span::styled(card.stage.clone(), Style::default().fg(Color::White)),
   200→    ]));
   201→
   202→    // ── Provider ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   203→    if let Some(ref provider) = card.provider {
   204→        lines.push(Line::from(vec![
   205→            Span::styled(
   206→                "Provider: ",
   207→                Style::default()
   208→                    .fg(Color::DarkGray)
   209→                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
   210→            ),
   211→            Span::styled(provider.clone(), Style::default().fg(Color::Cyan)),
   212→        ]));
   213→    }
   214→
   215→    // ── Elapsed ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   216→    if let Some(elapsed) = card.elapsed_s {
   217→        lines.push(Line::from(vec![
   218→            Span::styled(
   219→                "Elapsed: ",
   220→                Style::default()
   221→                    .fg(Color::DarkGray)
   222→                    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD),
   223→            ),
   224→            Span::styled(format_duration(elapsed), Style::default().fg(Color::White)),
   225→        ]));
   226→    }
   227→
   228→    // ── Priority ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   229→    if let Some(pri) = card.priority {
   230→        let (label, color) = match pri {
   231→            1 => ("Urgent", Color::Red),
   232→            2 => ("High", Color::Yellow),
   233→            3 => ("Normal", Color::White),
   234→            4 => ("Low", Color::DarkGray),
   235→            _ => ("Unknown", Color::Gray),
   236→        };
   237→        lines.push(Line::from(vec![
   238→            Span::styled(

+/// Level 3 — Full renderer (256-color, demoscene block headers).
+///
+/// Section headers use `░▒▓ LABEL ▓▒░` dithered fade filled to terminal width.
+/// Left-accent `▌` in state color; dice glyphs ⚀⚁⚂⚃⚄⚅ encode priority P1–P6.
+/// Progress bar: 16-cell `████░░░░` (U+2588 filled, U+2591 empty).
+/// Phase name + moon quarter glyph. Footer with same dithered pattern.
+use crate::acplan::half_circle_glyph;
+use crate::colors::{state_ansi, BOLD, DIM, RESET};
+
+use super::{CardRenderer, CardView, Stats};
+
+pub struct FullRenderer {
+    pub width: u16,
+}
+
+/// Dice glyph for priority 1–6: ⚀⚁⚂⚃⚄⚅.
+///
+/// Returns `None` for out-of-range or unset priority.
+fn dice_glyph(priority: Option<i64>) -> Option<char> {
+    match priority {
+        Some(1) => Some('⚀'),
+        Some(2) => Some('⚁'),
+        Some(3) => Some('⚂'),
+        Some(4) => Some('⚃'),

entries.content: The commit succeeded. Now let me update the subtask status in the implementation plan:

Subtask 3-2 is complete. Here's a summary of all changes made:

### Changes Summary

**Trait update (`render/mod.rs`):**
- Extended `CardRenderer::render_progress()` signature with two new params: `ac_done: Option<usize>` and `ac_total: Option<usize>`
- Updated `render_board()` call site to pass `card.ac_subtasks_done` and `card.ac_subtasks_total`

**Renderer updates (`full.rs`, `extended.rs`, `truecolor.rs`):**
- Added `use crate::acplan::half_circle_glyph;` import
- When `ac_done` and `ac_total` are `Some`: shows `"N/T"` count instead of `"N%"` percentage
- When AC plan data present: uses `half_circle_glyph()` (5 thresholds: ○◔◑◕●) instead of `moon_glyph()` (4 thresholds: ◔◑◕●)
- Format: `████████████░░░░  6/7   Phase 2: CLI + Docs  ◑`

**Non-target renderers (`dumb.rs`, `basic.rs`):**
- Added `_ac_done` and `_ac_total` params (ignored, these renderers don't support AC data)

**Kanban widget (`kanban.rs`):**
- Updated `build_progress_line()` with same N/T count and `half_circle_glyph` logic

**Data wiring (`acplan.rs`):**
- `enrich_card_view()` now populates `view.ac_subtasks_done` and `view.ac_subtasks_total` when plan data has subtasks

**Tests:** All 872 tests pass (0 failures).

1→# AC implementation plan progress in Quick Look + CLI
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→
     5→Auto-Claude's `implementation_plan.json` has per-phase subtask state
     6→(pending / in_progress / completed) that gives a clear picture of how far
     7→along a job is. Neither the Quick Look card preview nor `bop list` surface
     8→this — only raw `progress: Int` from `meta.json` is shown. The goal is to
     9→pipe AC's phase/subtask tree into the card's visual layer with zero extra
    10→tooling: block progress bars in the CLI, and a dedicated "Plan" tab in Quick
    11→Look alongside the existing Subtasks tab.
    12→
    13→## Data model
    14→
    15→### 1. `ac_spec_id` field in `Meta`
    16→
    17→Add to `crates/bop-core/src/lib.rs`:
    18→
    19→```rust
    20→pub ac_spec_id: Option<String>,   // e.g. "022"
    21→```
    22→
    23→This is the only link needed. Quick Look and CLI resolve the full path:
    24→
    25→```
    26→<git_root>/.auto-claude/specs/<ac_spec_id>-*/implementation_plan.json
    27→```
    28→
    29→Use a glob search (`read_dir` + prefix match on `<ac_spec_id>-`) to find the
    30→spec dir — do not hardcode the slug.
    31→
    32→### 2. How `ac_spec_id` gets written
    33→
    34→Two entry points:
    35→
    36→**dispatch.nu** — when `write_approval` fires before spawning a pane, also
    37→search `.cards/` (all state dirs) for a card whose `meta.json` `id` matches
    38→the spec slug or whose `title` contains the spec ID. If found, write
    39→`ac_spec_id` into that card's meta. This is the dogfood path where bop uses
    40→itself to track its own development.
    41→
    42→**Adapter convention** — if an adapter runs AC internally, it can write
    43→`ac_spec_id` to `meta.json` via `write_meta` before exiting.
    44→
    45→For cards with no AC spec, `ac_spec_id` is absent — no change to rendering.
    46→
    47→## CLI changes (`crates/bop-cli/src/main.rs` or status command)
    48→
    49→### `bop list` / `bop status` inline progress
    50→
    51→When a card has `ac_spec_id` set, load `implementation_plan.json` and render
    52→a compact one-liner after the card row:
    53→
    54→```
    55→⚙ 022-atomic-write-meta   running   claude   6m 14s
    56→  ████████████░░░░  6/7   Phase 2: CLI + Docs  ◑
    57→```
    58→
    59→Format:
    60→- Block bar: 16 chars, `█` per completed subtask fraction, `░` remainder
    61→- `N/T` count: completed subtasks / total subtasks across all phases
    62→- Current phase name (truncated to 24 chars)
    63→- Phase completion half-circle: `○◔◑◕●` (0% 25% 50% 75% 100%)
    64→  - U+25CB, U+25D4, U+25D1, U+25D5, U+25CF — all BMP-safe
    65→
    66→Half-circle thresholds (fraction of subtasks in current phase done):
    67→- 0 % → `○`
    68→- 1–33 % → `◔`
    69→- 34–66 % → `◑`
    70→- 67–99 % → `◕`
    71→- 100 % → `●`
    72→
    73→Colors: completed subtasks = green, in_progress = amber, pending = dim.
    74→Only shown for cards where `ac_spec_id` resolves to a readable file.
    75→
    76→### `bop status --watch` (spec 025)
    77→
    78→When the plan view is live, also reload `implementation_plan.json` on each
    79→notify event — it changes as the AC agent commits subtasks.
    80→
    81→## Quick Look changes (`macos/bop/PreviewViewController.swift`)
    82→
    83→### New tab: "Plan"
    84→
    85→Add `plan = "Plan"` to the `CardSection` enum (alongside `subtasks`).
    86→Show the tab only if `ac_spec_id` is set and `implementation_plan.json` loads.
    87→
    88→The Plan tab reads `implementation_plan.json` from the git root (walk up from
    89→the card dir via `../` until `.git` or `.auto-claude` is found).
    90→
    91→### New Codable structs
    92→
    93→```swift
    94→private struct AcPlan: Codable {
    95→    let phases: [AcPhase]
    96→}
    97→
    98→private struct AcPhase: Codable {
    99→    let id: String
   100→    let name: String
   101→    let subtasks: [AcSubtask]
   102→}
   103→
   104→private struct AcSubtask: Codable {
   105→    let id: String
   106→    let description: String
   107→    let status: String   // "pending" | "in_progress" | "completed"
   108→}
   109→```
   110→
   111→### Plan tab rendering
   112→
   113→Overall progress bar at top (same style as existing `subtasksTab` header):
   114→
   115→```
   116→8 of 12 subtasks complete          67%
   117→████████████░░░░░░░░
   118→```
   119→
   120→Then per-phase collapsible rows:
   121→
   122→```
   123→◉  Phase 1 — Crash Safety          ●●●●●  5/5
   124→   ✓ Make write_meta atomic
   125→   ✓ Make write_providers atomic
   126→   ✓ Add recover_orphans function
   127→   ✓ Call recover_orphans on startup
   128→   ✓ Add bop recover CLI command
   129→
   130→◔  Phase 2 — CLI + Docs            ●○○    1/3
   131→   ✓ Write result.md (in_progress)
   132→   ○ Run make check
   133→   ○ Final review
   134→```
   135→
   136→Glyph legend (BMP-safe, existing SF Symbols for Quick Look):
   137→- Phase status icon: `circle.fill` (done), `circle.lefthalf.filled` (partial),
   138→  `circle` (not started) — SF Symbols, no font dep
   139→- Subtask row: `checkmark.circle.fill` (completed, green),
   140→  `arrow.triangle.2.circlepath` (in_progress, amber), `circle` (pending, dim)
   141→  — matches the existing `subtasksTab` icon style
   142→
   143→Phase name row background: match existing card row style
   144→(`Color.black.opacity(0.15)` + `RoundedRectangle` border).
   145→
   146→### `BopCardMeta` addition
   147→
   148→```swift
   149→let acSpecId: String?
   150→
   151→enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
   152→    // ... existing keys ...
   153→    case acSpecId = "ac_spec_id"
   154→}
   155→```
   156→
   157→Load `AcPlan` lazily when the Plan tab is first shown — do not block the
   158→initial card render. Use a `@State var acPlan: AcPlan? = nil` loaded in
   159→`.task { }` on the tab view.
   160→
   161→Git root discovery: walk parent dirs from `cardURL.deletingLastPathComponent()`
   162→until a dir containing `.auto-claude` is found, max 6 levels. Cache the result.
   163→
   164→## Acceptance
   165→
   166→- `Meta` has `ac_spec_id: Option<String>` field (serde rename `ac_spec_id`)
   167→- `bop list` renders block bar + phase half-circle for cards with `ac_spec_id`
   168→- `bop status --watch` reloads plan on file changes
   169→- Quick Look shows "Plan" tab when `ac_spec_id` resolves to a readable plan
   170→- Plan tab shows overall block bar + per-phase subtask list with SF Symbol icons
   171→- Git root discovery walks up from card dir (max 6 levels)
   172→- `make check` passes
   173→- `output/result.md` with ASCII screenshot of CLI output and description of QL tab
   174→
